item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You’re close, though I don’t want to give it all away haha. I’ve been away from writing from some time, it is to be known that this SCP will be getting a major grammatical upgrade and an added section for others to add their reports of test games and such. I’ve just been having a lot going on stopping me from doing so. So there will be more info and further clarity in the future but I am not saying it is incomplete.

As for the final game time. I wanted to really emphasize the purpose of the “Gamemaster”. Without spewing it all over. I wish I had more know how to make text fade into something else on these pages but the idea was more. You go to view this classified document, okay level 5 security blah blah. But all you see is [Data Expunged] not as like a filler or replacement for data but that is what is actually written. That then in of itself is a security measure created similarly to SCP foundation ones but is clearly a grade above. Which reveals the true first document. The document gives more insight into this “Game Time” and is not supposed be entirely understood as of this moment. What is to be know is that game time is set to start when the universe is at its unstoppable end. Like I said you’re close, but not just there.

I will be adding more to this and was planning to piece another SCP and a few tales together into one larger story involving the “Gamemaster” and what was mentioned in the SCP file. I have much bigger plans I just didn’t know if anyone was interested in reading them is all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So it seems like administration is running the game, administration is that of the SCP Foundation (since they use the term XK-class,) mere Agents are the players of this game (or at least pawns in it,) and ALL of this was apparently true BEFORE a GameMaster was tricked into activating Game Time to save our Universe, since the log at the end references events outside of and in fact adjudicating that Universe.

Am I close?

If this is not the intended read, is the use of terms like agent, administration, and XK- intended as a deliberate red herring?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yea I need someone to look over it more closely for the grammar, my eye just doesn't catch it. Yea I thought about others wanting to make logs for this SCP but not exactly sure how I would go about it. So if you have suggestions i'm all for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a few grammatical errors, but I love the concept and testing logs! Perhaps make an experiment log similar to SCP-914, as I believe there are plenty of people who would like to write “games” (like me). Great job! +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yea, I was honestly a bit worried about having that document at the end but I think it works well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The SCP is a cool concept, the test logs and everything else are interesting, and the allusion to something bigger going on at the end is stimulating. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just changed it so both say 3-4123. I had a wrong understanding of formatting that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Given an input, normally in the form of an object, SCP-4123 will create a game based upon it. The game is fully immersive and is designated SCP-4123-1. The game is created and maintained through anomalous phenomena directed by the intelligence inhabiting SCP-XXXX. These phenomena include spatial displacement, the creation of objects and creatures and low-level reality manipulation.

You accidentally left an SCP-XXXX in this paragraph.
Thought you might want to change that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You could put "or unless given permission by the site director"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was trying to show that in order to view the file you must at least be level 3, but as a level 3 you have to get a level 4’s approval to access the file. I though that was the right way to show that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At the top it says 3-4123 clearance not 4-4123
